prerequisites: A SparkPost account. US and EU accounts operate independently; an account created in the US region cannot be used in the EU region and vice versa.
An API key generated in the SparkPost dashboard, granted permissions to read the data you intend to extract.
Knowledge of which region your account uses, since this determines the api_prefix value (US or EU host).
A start_date marking the earliest point from which to begin syncing data.

authMethods.name: API key
authMethods.summary: SparkPost authenticates every request with an API key sent in the Authorization HTTP header. Provide the key in the api_key field. Generate the key from the SparkPost dashboard and grant it the permissions required to read the resources you want to extract.

setupSteps.title: Create an API key
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the SparkPost dashboard and create a new API key. Grant it the permissions needed to read the data you plan to sync. Copy the key value when it is shown, as it cannot be retrieved again later.

setupSteps.title: Identify your region (api_prefix)
setupSteps.body: Determine whether your account is in the US or EU region, since the two are independent. The US API is served from api.sparkpost.com and the EU API from api.eu.sparkpost.com. Set the api_prefix config field to match your account's region so requests reach the correct host.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter your API key in the api_key field, set api_prefix to your region, and set start_date to the earliest date from which data should be retrieved. The key is sent in the Authorization header on each request to the SparkPost API.
